data.title: United States Cellular Q4 Non-GAAP Earnings, Revenue Decline
data.description:
United States Cellular reported Q4 non-GAAP earnings Friday of $0.05 per diluted share, down from $0.16 a year earlier.

Two analysts polled by FactSet expected a loss of $0.03.

Total operating revenue for the quarter ended Dec. 31 was $970.0 million, down from $1.00 billion a year earlier.

Three analysts surveyed by FactSet expected $960.3 million.

The mobile telecommunications company's shares were up more than 1% in recent premarket activity.

data.title: NatWest to Redeem EUR1.5 Billion of Notes due March 2026
data.description:
British lender NatWest Group will redeem its outstanding 1.5 billion euros of fixed-to-floating rate notes due March 2026 on March 2, 2025.

The bonds will be purchased at par plus accrued interest, according to a Friday release.

data.description: Cenovus Energy's Canadian stock has an average rating of buy and mean price target of CA$29.21, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

data.title: Japan Pulp and Paper Logs 16% Lower Profit for Nine Months to December 2024
data.description:
Japan Pulp and Paper recorded a profit attributable to owners of the parent of 6.98 billion yen for the nine months ended Dec. 31, 2024, 15.7% lower than 8.27 billion yen logged for the same period a year ago.

The company's earnings per share for the reporting period decreased to 56.49 yen from 61.56 yen per share a year ago, according to a delayed Wednesday filing with the Tokyo Exchange.

Revenue of the paper trading and real estate company came in at 405.5 billion yen, 1.5% lower than 411.6 billion yen a year earlier.

It forecasted an attributable profit of 9 billion yen, a basic EPS of 73.06 yen, an operating profit of 16 billion yen, and a final dividend of 12.50 yen per share for the fiscal year ending March. 31.

data.title: Holcim to Rebrand North American Business as Amrize After Spin-Off
data.description:
Holcim said Friday it will rebrand its North American building material business as Amrize following the planned spin-off.

The Swiss building materials company plans to list Amrize on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker AMRZ, with an additional listing on Switzerland's SIX Swiss Exchange.

The rebranding will take effect upon the spin-off's completion, which is expected in the first half of 2025, subject to shareholder and regulatory approvals.

data.title: Top Premarket Decliners
data.description:
Inspirato shares slumped 54% pre-bell Friday after Thursday's 4.1% increase.

TransAct Technologies shares retreated 30% following a muted session.

Eupraxia Pharmaceuticals shares fell 27%, erasing Thursday's gains.

Junee shares decreased 21% after a 5.1% increase in the previous session.

Algoma Steel shares were down 21%, adding to Thursday's 1% loss.

data.title: Sunstone Hotel Investors' Adjusted Funds From Operations, Revenue Fall, 2025 Non-GAAP FFO Outlook Set
data.description:
Sunstone Hotel Investors reported Q4 adjusted funds from operations Friday of $0.16 per diluted share, down from $0.19 a year earlier.

Analysts polled by FactSet expected $0.12.

Revenue for the quarter ended Dec. 31 was $214.8 million, compared with $219.2 million a year earlier.

Analysts surveyed by FactSet expected $208.9 million.

The company said it expects 2025 adjusted funds from operations of $0.86 to $0.98 per diluted share.

Analysts surveyed by FactSet expect $0.86.

data.title: Iron Ore Holds at 7-Month High
data.description:
Prices for iron ore cargoes were at CNY 840 per tonne in February, the highest in seven months, amid bets that Chinese ferrous metal demand is heading toward a recovery.

The latest data showed that new government bonds issued by Chinese authorities totaled CNY 693 billion in January, more than double from the previous year, to indicate that officials are starting to follow through on earlier pledges to stimulate the country’s slowing economy.

This magnified optimism that the housing crisis will slow after the decline in prices bottomed last year and softened to a six-month low in February.

Additionally, local authorities reportedly started to process of buying property from major distressed developers, signaling willingness to aid the sector that is among the world’s top steel consumers.

In the meantime, major iron ore producer BHP noted that signs of economic recovery in China and rate cuts by global central banks this year is due to revive demand for precious metals.

data.title: Royal Philips, Mass General Collaborate to Enhance Patient Care Through Live AI-Powered Tools
data.description:
Royal Philips and Mass General Brigham said Friday they signed a new partnership to develop and deploy advanced data infrastructure and artificial intelligence to enhance patient care.

Financial terms were not disclosed.

The parties intend to combine their specialties to gather detailed data from medical devices and other sources to form a single, real-time data and insights system, they added.

Mass General will integrate its biomedical engineering team, clinical sub-specialists, digital teams, and internal AI business unit with Philips' research and business teams, the companies said.

data.title: Endeavour Silver Surface Drilling at Bolanitos Intersects High-Grade Mineralization
data.description:
Endeavour Silver on Friday said recent surface drilling intersected high-grade gold and silver mineralization at the Bolanitos Mine's La Luz vein in Mexico.

Highlights include 542 grams per tonne (gpt) silver equivalent over a 1.02 m estimated true width (ETW) in hole LZ46-3; and 1,258 gpt silver equivalent over a 1.62 m ETW, in hole LZ48-1.

The 2024/2025 program focused on drilling the La Luz vein, and the high grade results provides opportunities to replace reserves, increase resources and extend mine life, Endeavour added.

"For more than 17 years, our exploration success has been integral to the ongoing operations at Bolanitos," said Chief Executive Dan Dickson. "The latest results highlight our focus on efficient, cost-effective exploration, which continues to extend the mine's life."

Endeavour Silver was last seen down US$0.07, to US$1.68, in U.S. pre-market trading.

data.title: Update: Walt Disney's ESPN, Major League Baseball to End Media Rights Partnership
data.description:
(Updates with ESPN's statement on X.)

Walt Disney' ESPN confirmed that it has opted out of its partnership with Major League Baseball, or MLB, in a Friday post on social media platform X.

ESPN said in the post that it remains open to exploring other ways to serve the league's fans after 2025.

The Wall Street Journal reported the end of the parties' media rights partnership and said ESPN was due to pay the league $550 million per year from 2026 to 2028 in airing rights.

MLB Commissioner Rob Manfred told team owners that ESPN made an "aggressive effort to reduce rights fees", according to a letter seen by WSJ.

Manfred said that the league had "not been pleased with the minimal coverage that MLB has received on ESPN's platforms," and that it does not think pay TV is the best platform for its content and plans to explore opportunities with a new broadcast or streaming partner.

Manfred also showed interest in selling a national streaming package that would centralize local media rights after MLB's deals with Fox and Turner Sports expire in 2028, according to the report.

MLB did not immediately respond to MT Newswires' request for comment.

data.title: Market Chatter: Foxconn Looks to Partner With Honda, Other Japan Automakers in EV Space
data.description:
Taiwanese electronics manufacturer Foxconn is looking to partner with Honda Motor together with Nissan Motor and Mitsubishi Motors in the electric vehicle space, Nikkei reported Friday, citing a source familiar with the matter.

Foxconn Chairman Young Liu said he is looking to collaborate on the design and manufacture of EVs on a contract basis, although the company does not plan to establish its own automobile brand, the report said.

Foxconn, Honda, Nissan and Mitsubishi did not immediately reply to MT Newswires' request for comment.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: UScellular Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2024 Results
data.description:
United States Cellular Corporation, a leading wireless telecommunications provider, has released its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year of 2024. The company reported a decrease in total operating revenues and net income compared to the previous year, alongside several strategic initiatives and corporate developments.

Financial Highlights

For the fourth quarter of 2024, UScellular reported total operating revenues of $970 million, a 3% decrease from $1,000 million in the same period of 2023. Net income attributable to UScellular shareholders was $5 million, down from $14 million in the fourth quarter of 2023. Diluted earnings per share (EPS) for the quarter were $0.05, compared to $0.16 in the previous year.

For the full year 2024, total operating revenues were $3,770 million, a 3% decline from $3,906 million in 2023. The company reported a net loss attributable to shareholders of $39 million, or $(0.46) per diluted share, compared to a net income of $54 million, or $0.63 per diluted share, in 2023. Excluding a third-quarter license impairment of $136 million, net income for 2024 was $63 million, or $0.71 per diluted share.

Business and Operational Highlights

UScellular saw improvements in its wireless operating results, with reduced postpaid and prepaid net losses and churn rates. The company also reported a 27% growth in fixed wireless customers. Cash flows from operating activities and free cash flow increased year-over-year, and the ongoing deployment of the 5G mid-band network provided additional capacity and faster speeds for customers.

Strategic Initiatives and Corporate Developments

In 2024, UScellular announced several transactions as part of its strategic alternatives review. These included a Securities Purchase Agreement with T-Mobile to sell its wireless operations and select spectrum assets, expected to close in mid-2025. Additionally, the company entered into License Purchase Agreements with Verizon, Nsight Spectrum, Nex-Tech Wireless, and AT&T for the sale of various spectrum licenses.

During 2024, UScellular repurchased 939,999 of its Common Shares for $55 million.

Management's Perspective

Laurent Therivel, UScellular President and CEO, commented, "In 2024, UScellular made significant progress in enhancing shareholder value, while remaining steadfast in its mission of connecting people to what matters most. We also maintained strong financial discipline resulting in solid growth in profitability and free cash flow. While subscriber results remained negative, we saw meaningful improvement in postpaid and prepaid additions in the third and fourth quarters of 2024. We intend to build on this momentum and will continue to invest in our customers and our network while working towards closing the transactions that we have previously announced."

Future Outlook

Due to the pending transaction with T-Mobile, UScellular is not providing financial guidance for 2025.

SEC Filing: UNITED STATES CELLULAR CORP [ USM ] - 8-K - Feb. 21, 2025

data.title: Telephone and Data Systems, Inc. Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2024 Results
data.description:
Telephone and Data Systems, Inc. (TDS) has released its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year of 2024. The company, which provides wireless, broadband, video, and voice services through its subsidiaries UScellular and TDS Telecom, reported a decrease in total operating revenues and net income compared to the previous year.

Financial Highlights

For the fourth quarter of 2024, TDS reported total operating revenues of $1,240 million, a 6% decrease from $1,313 million in the same period of 2023. The net loss attributable to TDS common shareholders was $(11) million, or $(0.10) per diluted share, compared to a net loss of $(523) million, or $(4.64) per diluted share, in the fourth quarter of 2023. For the full year 2024, total operating revenues were $4,964 million, down 4% from $5,160 million in 2023. The net loss attributable to TDS common shareholders for the year was $(97) million, or $(0.85) per diluted share, compared to $(569) million, or $(5.06) per diluted share, in 2023.

Business and Operational Highlights

UScellular, a subsidiary of TDS, announced multiple transactions related to its strategic alternatives review, including a significant transaction with T-Mobile and four spectrum transactions with various mobile network operators. The company also reported improvements in wireless operating results, with postpaid and prepaid net losses and churn rates showing positive trends. Fixed wireless customers grew by 27%, and cash flows from operating activities and free cash flow increased year over year. UScellular continued its 5G mid-band network deployment, enhancing capacity and speed for customers.

TDS Telecom exceeded its full-year 2024 fiber address goal by delivering 129,000 marketable fiber service addresses. The company expanded its footprint by 6%, increasing total service addresses to 1.8 million. Residential broadband connections grew by 2%, and residential revenue per connection increased by 5%. Total wireline expansion residential revenues grew to $114 million, up from $75 million in 2023.

Strategic Initiatives and Corporate Developments

In 2024, TDS and UScellular entered into a Securities Purchase Agreement to sell UScellular's wireless operations and select spectrum assets to T-Mobile, with the transaction expected to close in mid-2025. Additionally, UScellular entered into agreements with Verizon Communications, Inc., Nsight Spectrum, LLC, Nex-Tech Wireless, LLC, and New Cingular Wireless PCS, LLC (AT&T) for the sale of various spectrum licenses. Due to the pending transaction with T-Mobile, UScellular is not providing 2025 financial guidance.

Management's Perspective

Walter C. D. Carlson, TDS President and CEO, commented on the company's progress in 2024, highlighting the strategic review of alternatives at UScellular and investments in high-quality networks. Carlson emphasized the ongoing fiber expansion at TDS Telecom and the focus on operational priorities and growing the tower business at UScellular.

Future Outlook

TDS Telecom provided guidance for 2025, estimating total operating revenues between $1,030 million and $1,070 million, adjusted OIBDA between $310 million and $350 million, and adjusted EBITDA between $320 million and $360 million. Capital expenditures are expected to range from $375 million to $425 million. The company plans to continue its fiber expansion and participate in the FCC’s Enhanced Alternative Connect America Cost Model program, aiming to bring a fiber-rich network to over 150,000 new marketable service addresses throughout the country.

SEC Filing: TELEPHONE & DATA SYSTEMS INC /DE/ [ TDS ] - 8-K - Feb. 21, 2025

data.title: Benitec Biopharma Price Target Maintained With a $28.00/Share by HC Wainwright & Co.
Astronics Ordered to Pay Lufthansa Technik $11.9 Million in Patent Infringement Case
data.description:
Astronics said Friday it has been ordered by a UK court to pay $11.9 million to Lufthansa Technik to settle a patent infringement dispute.

The company said any additional amounts related to matters peripheral to the case will be determined at a hearing scheduled for next month.

Astronics said it expects payment of the final liability to be required in Q2, and that any appeal would likely be heard in early 2026.

Astronics' shares were down 2% in premarket trading.

data.title: Mexico GDP Contraction Confirmed at 3-Year High
data.description:
The Mexican gross domestic product contracted by 0.6% from the previous quarter in the final three months of 2024, trimming the 1.1% expansion in the previous period and in line with preliminary estimates to confirm the sharp miss of expectations of a 0.2% drop.

It was the sharpest contraction since the third quarter of 2021, aligned with signals from the central bank that larger rate cuts may be appropriate this year, especially if the US government follows through with tariff threats.

Output from primary activities plunged by 8.5% from the previous quarter, while output from industry fell by 1.5%.

In turn, services output edged higher by 0.2%.

On an annual basis, the Mexican GDP expanded by 0.5% in the fourth quarter, revised lower from the preliminary reading of 0.6%.

For the full year, the Mexican economy expanded by 1.2%.
